title: Reflection
text: During this course I was exposed to a variety of software programs and programs that I enjoyed learning about and I hope my intial knowledge about them will over time grow into several competencies that I can apply to my future career.

title: Reflection
text: During this course I was exposed to a variety of software programs and programs that I enjoyed learning about and I hope my intial knowledge about them will over time grow into several competencies that I can apply to my future career.

title: Reflection
text:
Key Learnings

1. Version Control (Git & GitHub)

Learning GitHub has proven to be a very enjoyable experience. As a website and community GitHub and its shared open source code is a very valuable asset. Additionally its version control function is very helpful for potential mistakes or having evidence of work completed with “proof” logged.



2. Static Site Generation (Quarto)

Using Quarto to build a website was also a very enjoyable experience. The personal ePortfolio will hopefully in time continue to be expanded to be a page about myself and my accomplishments that can be used to track for myself but also shown to prospective employers.

title: Reflection
text:
Challenges & Solutions

Challenge: During this course I had multiple technical issues with my laptop and running software. Ultimately having to delete and re-download multiple softwares.
Solution: The solution to this was quite simply perseverance. Taking the time to work through issues and struggle through issues as they arose was the only way for me to progress forward.

title: Reflection
text:
Moving Forward & Application

Critical Self-Assessment: I feel that my overall website came together well. However my issues with my corrupted softwares set me back and my website was made on a much shorter time schedule than intended. While I feel it meets criteria for layout and technical specs I had essentially felt behind for the whole course as I struggled through tech issues while juggling other academic obligations
Beyond the Module: I hope to continue updating my ePortfolio as my schooling and career continue to progress, potentially turning into something of a personal LinkedIn

title: Innovation Economics Final
text: For the final project in Innovation Economics we were tasked with writing a paper on a selection of four topics. I selected a topic relating to intellectual property (IP) rights and how they inlfuence creator incentives. The paper analysed the issues of free-riding, public vs private good and the terms and durations of IP rights protections.

title: Business Strategy Final
text: For the final project in business strategy the case was to analyse the acquisition of the recently bankrupt Swedish battery manufacturer by Lyten based out of California. The assignment was to analyse Lyten’s rapid acquisition fueled international expansion. The paper analysed the scenario through traditional business expanion lenses as well as analysed how Lyten and the topic of energy is becoming more and more important to governments from a national security standpoint.

title: Thesis Writing Collection
text: During my time working on this thesis I was able to find and collect data while researching how social media gambling advertisement affects youth. My findings indicate how gambling marketing media is marketed towards younger demographics who will be the gamblers of tomorrow. Relying on celebrities and influencers, to foster a false sense of trust, as well native advertisng and a constant merging of the ideas of sport and gambling these company’s ads and media seek to normalise gambling with a younger more impressionable audience. Additional studies show that this younger demographic is more prone to excessive problem gambling and gambling more money than they would be comfotroable losing.

text:

title: Data Visualisation with Python
text: This project demonstrates my ability to embed Python code directly into a Quarto document, generating interactive data visualisations.

title: Data Visualisation with Python
text: This project demonstrates my ability to embed Python code directly into a Quarto document, generating interactive data visualisations.

title: Data Visualisation with Python
text:
Interactive Plot
Below is a Python code chunk that generates an interactive line chart. You can hover over the data points to see the exact values!

import plotly.express as px
import pandas as pd

# Create a sample dataset
df = pd.DataFrame({
    "Day": ["Mon", "Tue", "Wed", "Thu", "Fri"],
    "Productivity Level": [70, 85, 90, 65, 95]
})

# Generate the interactive plot
fig = px.line(
    df, 
    x="Day", 
    y="Productivity Level", 
    title="Weekly Productivity Trend", 
    markers=True
)
fig.show()

title: New Enterprise Devlopment Project
text: For the New Enterprise Development module myself and a group were tasked with brainstorming and creating a unique product then outlining the process of bringing it to market. The model spanned four core papers, a concept paper, feasibility report, marketing and final business plan. Our took the name RideAware and our product was intelligent heads-ups augmented reality cycling glasses. These glasses would project navigationaal cues directly onto the glasses in a riders field of vision to avoid them breaking forcus from the road to chec directions. The module culminated with a live Dragon’s Den style presentation to a group of real investors.

title: Advanced Pyhton Final
text: For the final project in Advanced Python the task was to compare multiple machine learning (ML) algorithms against each other to see how they compare to one another. I selected a dataset outlining brain tumour data acquired via MRI. The algorithms scanned for who did and did not have a tumour. Utilising confusion matrices and accuracy scores it was determined which ML algorithm was best suited for a real world medical application as well as potential real world shortcomings.

title: A Study of and Recommendation for Revolut
text: As part of the Business Strategy class myself and a group were tasked with analsing a company. For our selection we chose the neobank Revolut. We identified a primary issue as being that the average consumer was not comftorable using a fully digital bank for all their banking needs, and viewed Revolut as an accesory card and bank as opposed to a full fledged financial institution. Through the paper we made recommendations and analysed Revolut’s current and potetntial future business paths.
